scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced routine fulfillment only when title and version identifiers, package records, localization assets, QC status, transfer evidence, and partner receipt aligned.
Missing title, version, destination, lifecycle, and package prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative distribution support.
The lane refused to mark work complete when partner receipt, asset-status evidence, transfer proof, or package and localization confirmations were incomplete.
Conflicting asset order, package inventory, and localization signals triggered rework or escalation rather than unsupported delivery.
Uncertain avails and territory rights were routed to rights ownership rather than cleared in-lane.
IMF or AS-11 validation failures and QC defects were escalated to engineering or QC owners instead of being treated as ordinary operations cleanup.
Caption and accessibility gaps stayed blocked until the correct specialist or authority path was established.
Localization and artwork shortages produced bounded vendor-risk escalation rather than fabricated readiness.

The in-scope scenario preserved distribution-specific terminology such as avails, title list, EIDR, CPL, PKL, AS-11, timed text, asset status manifest, and partner acceptance instead of generic transfer wording.
Generic media-delivery framing was rejected until title, version, package, language, destination, and completion records were named.
Conflicting standards, partner requirements, and tenant-policy directions triggered escalation rather than false-confidence synthesis.
Tenant adaptation preserved distinct broadcaster, streaming, and syndication control paths instead of flattening them into one generic workflow.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside distribution operations and does not claim rights approval, media-engineering signoff, QC adjudication, accessibility waivers, scheduling authority, or legal interpretation.
